name: GoFundMe
hero: Hey, GoFundMe. I built this page to hold job-specific notes on how each piece of my work connects to what you need for the Senior Product Designer role. I'm still adding work examples, so there'll be more here over the next few days.
now: TBD: A note for the GoFundMe team about what I'm focused on right now and how it lines up with the Senior Product Designer role on Heart.

milestones.2026-job-search: GoFundMe has actually shown up for people I love - it helped my wife after a neighbor burned down their apartment building, and it's raised money for a bunch of my friends when they needed it. So this one's personal. And the role is a real fit: I've spent years being the person who can design the system, build the product, and help a team raise its bar, all at once.
milestones.world-ia-day-2026: GoFundMe wants someone who can lead, run workshops, and represent the company out loud. That's exactly what chairing this conference was, and it's work I'm genuinely good at and enjoy.
milestones.list-at-ease: GoFundMe wants someone working closely with product and engineering, someone who can see the whole story. I know how to listen and I don't just talk the talk. I'll jump in and get my hands dirty and help clear the way and support all roles.
milestones.aicad-2024: GoFundMe is built on community and mission. I love being part of that - sharing the process, the mission, and the outcomes. Getting out to speak, meet people, and build relationships is something I genuinely enjoy, and something GoFundMe seems to want in a designer.
milestones.open-office-hours: GoFundMe has a huge range of people and projects to keep connected. I know how to meet people where they're at, figure out how to help, and push them to be their best.
milestones.better-life: GoFundMe is built on people helping each other - and this project ran on the same spirit. 100% of profits went to charity, and the whole thing was designed to help people in their actual lives, not just their deals. Making something like that work isn't just pretty UI - it's full-on service design at every level.
milestones.equivalent-design: GoFundMe serves a huge range of people, often at their most vulnerable - so accessibility and empathy aren't just a checklist. I've done the deep R&D, and I teach screen-reader-first design as a way of keeping this in mind all the way through.
milestones.css-tricks-article: GoFundMe's Heart is already established. I might evangelize for a methodology I believe in, but what matters most is how the team actually uses it - there's no one 'right way.' Still, I'm always looking for new ways of seeing things, and for ways to help the team communicate better and carry less friction and debt.
milestones.pe-founded: GoFundMe runs on triads and cross-functional teams - which only click when designers, engineers, and PMs actually share a language. Getting people from totally different backgrounds to communicate and build together is the whole thing I spent six years figuring out.
milestones.mentoring: GoFundMe is exactly the kind of place I've helped people land. I've mentored a lot of designers and developers in SF into their first role - or helped them hold onto one through a rocky start.
milestones.school-loop: GoFundMe already has a team and a system that work. I'm good at coming in and making things better from the inside - working with people, not around them, without disrupting what's already good.
milestones.agency-work: GoFundMe is likely a more established product machine, but I got my experience in the fast-paced world of agencies - so I can work with any setting and set of constraints.
milestones.nouveau-studios: Fun fact - while at Nouveau, we spec'd out a full GoFundMe competitor, and that's where I first worked through UX diagrams with one of the other designers.
